steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank sits inside the noun phrase 'a New Historicist analysis ______ on historical context.' The sentence already has a main verb: 'might yield.' The blank must modify the noun 'analysis' without creating a second main verb in the same clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — would be focusing: finite verb phrase. Adding it creates two main verbs ('would be focusing' and 'might yield') in one clause — a run-on. ✗
  • Choice B — would focus: also a finite verb. Same run-on problem. ✗
  • Choice C — focuses: finite verb. Again produces two main verbs. ✗
  • Choice D — focusing: present participle. Non-finite, so it modifies 'analysis' as a participial phrase and lets 'might yield' remain the sole main verb. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
